title: New Cohen-Lenstra heuristics by constructing measures from moments
speaker: Will Sawin
abstract: The Cohen-Lenstra heuristics give predictions for the distribution of the class groups of a random quadratic number field. Cohen and Martinet generalized them to predict the distribution of the class groups of random extensions of a fixed base field, but Malle pointed out that these predictions have errors arising from the roots of unity in the base field. We give amended predictions that account for the influence of roots of unity.   Our predictions are based on a result which produces a formula for the

proof_stages.label: Classical Cohen-Lenstra problem and failed generalizations
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins with the Cohen-Lenstra distribution for p-parts of imaginary quadratic class groups, explains the broader Cohen-Martinet program for Galois extensions with Z_p[Gamma]-module structure, and identifies roots of unity and nonabelian generalizations as sources of failure for older formulas.

proof_stages.label: Moment-first strategy for heuristics
proof_stages.summary: Instead of guessing class-group distributions directly, the speaker proposes conjecturing all moments given by expected numbers of surjections to finite Z_p[Gamma]-modules and then reconstructing the probability measure from those moments.

proof_stages.label: Predicted formulas for p-torsion distributions
proof_stages.summary: The speaker writes explicit conjectural formulas for cyclic cubic and cyclic septic examples and then a general p-torsion formula in terms of irreducible F_p-representations of Gamma, including corrections depending on roots of unity and infinite places.

proof_stages.label: Infinite linear moment problem
proof_stages.summary: All finite Z_p[Gamma]-modules are enumerated, the unknown distribution is encoded as a vector mu, the moment data as a vector m, and the relation m=A mu is expressed through the infinite matrix A whose entries count surjections.

proof_stages.label: Möbius formula for the inverse matrix
proof_stages.summary: To justify mu=A^{-1}m, the formal triangular inverse of the surjection matrix is identified with a Möbius-function formula on submodule posets, whose support on small p-torsion extensions gives the tail control needed for absolute convergence and exchange of infinite sums.

proof_stages.label: Abstract categorical reconstruction theorem
proof_stages.summary: The reconstruction argument is lifted from modules to a broad class of categories whose quotient posets form finite modular lattices with a Jordan-Hölder length, covering finite groups, rings, modules, Lie algebras, and related structures needed for future nonabelian heuristics.

proof_stages.label: Tightness and no escape of mass
proof_stages.summary: In the closing questions the speaker explains that the theorem also controls escape of mass: convergence of sufficiently many moments forces tightness and allows the limiting moments to equal the moments of the limiting measure, by an inductive dominated-convergence argument.

examples.name: Cyclic septic extensions and dependent F8-pieces
examples.note: For gamma=Z/7 and p=2, the class group splits into two dual F8-pieces whose exponents must remain close, revealing the new dependence phenomenon that invalidates older independence-based Cohen-Martinet-type guesses.
examples.engagement_reason: for cyclic septic extensions at p=2 the speaker decomposes the module into two F8-pieces, writes explicit probability formulas, and analyzes the dependence between the two exponents

passes.reason: The cyclic septic F8-splitting is used to exhibit dependence between representation pieces, contradicting the earlier independent-piece assumption.
